relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks the boundaries of inherited land.
Canaan is mapped as a future tribal inheritance.
The southern line begins beside Edom and the Salt Sea.
The boundary bends through Zin, Kadesh-barnea, Hazar-addar, and Azmon.
It reaches the wadi of Egypt and the sea.
The Great Sea forms the western edge.
The northern line rises from the sea toward Mount Hor.
It passes the entrance of Hamath toward Zedad.
Ziphron and Hazar-enan complete the north.
The eastern line begins at Hazar-enan and Shepham.
It descends past Riblah toward Chinnereth.
Jordan carries the line back to the Salt Sea.
Nine and a half tribes will receive land by lot within these borders.
Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh have already received east of Jordan.
Their inheritance faces sunrise beyond Jericho.
YHWH names those who will oversee the division.
Eleazar and Joshua hold shared responsibility.
Every tribe sends a leader into the process.
Caleb represents Judah.
Shemuel represents Simeon.
Elidad represents Benjamin.
Bukki represents Dan.
Hanniel represents Manasseh.
Kemuel represents Ephraim.
Elizaphan represents Zebulun.
Paltiel represents Issachar.
Ahihud represents Asher.
Pedahel represents Naphtali.
The land division is entrusted to a council rather than a single hand.

contemplative_note: Numbers 34 turns land into lines, but lines are never innocent. Its healthiest internal movement is shared administration; its dangerous reception is treating ancient sacred geography as permission to displace living people today.
